title: trifolium rugulosum
intro: Trifolium rugulosum, also known as the little-barred clover or rough clover, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and small, pinkish-white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Trifolium rugulosum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, while also providing a natural source of antioxidants and essential nutrients. Herbal preparations of Trifolium rugulosum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
